title: Longitudinal EEG Test-Retest Reliability in Healthy Individuals

funding_references.funder_name: Comité para el Desarrollo de la Investigación - CODI, Universidad de Antioquia
Comité para el Desarrollo de la Investigación (CODI)
Comité para el Desarrollo de la Investigación (CODI), Universidad de Antioquia

description: This longitudinal dataset comprises resting-state EEG recordings from 43 healthy adults collected across four sessions over approximately two years (mean inter-session interval: 7.2 months). The dataset was designed to evaluate the test-retest reliability of quantitative EEG measures using automated preprocessing techniques, including independent component analysis and wavelet-enhanced artifact removal. It enables investigation of neural component stability, relative power in regions of interest, and the longitudinal consistency of EEG-derived biomarkers in healthy populations.
methods_description: Resting-state EEG data were collected from 43 healthy adults across four sessions spanning approximately two years with an average interval of 7.2 months between sessions. Data were processed using an automated preprocessing pipeline incorporating independent component analysis and wavelet-enhanced artifact removal. EEG signals were recorded in BIDS-compliant format with associated metadata describing recording parameters.
